reviews.review_text:
It would have been a decent RPG had the encounter design not started increasingly relying on immersion-breaking metaknowledge cheese strats as the game went on. Turn one TPKs and gimmick fights are plentiful on Tactician (Act 4 is riddled with them) or you just sometimes lose automatically if you enter the fight directly from dialogue, so you reload the fight knowing about it in advance and only then can you build your strategy, which usually includes stupid pre-buffing and setting up the field while your enemies just stand there doing nothing or you teleport someone far away. Winning strategies made me feel like I was exploiting the game, but the enemies also cheat constantly and can start with 3 times your AP, especially the bosses, which means they can often just kill a party member or two on the first turn if they beat your Initiative. Classic on the other hand is fairly straightforward, but rather lacking in challenge in all those fights that are not just stupid gimmicks, as the AI isn't smart enough nor does it have the stats to survive well-made builds. I would have loved a middle ground between those two difficulties.

The game is also extremely linear because of the artificial gating of areas by level, which means you are forced to do most quests for EXP if you want to progress through the game without abusing one-shot cheese mechanics. The vast stat differences mean being even one level under the enemies' can put you in normally unwinnable situations. 

The plot is a massive slog for how simple it is, the writing is inconsistent and constantly switches between corny humor and grimdark eternal suffering. Everything is so video-gamey, and it is genuinely difficult to RP a consistent character and get immersed in the setting because of the underwhelming dialogue options and the unavoidable save/load hindsight many fights rely on, having to use knowledge my character never could have had to get by.
The inventory management and bartering is absolutely abysmal and lacking in the most basic QoL features, which only wastes your time and ruins the pacing even more. 

The exponentially scaling loot system essentially requiring you to gear up your whole party of FOUR every time you level up is more boring non-gameplay that should never have been in the game. Having to buy or steal spells (your main means of winning any fight) is also incredibly annoying at the start.
All of those things artifically prolong the game, making you spend several hours of your playthrough doing tedious vendor busywork instead of engaging with whatever fun the game has to offer.

As for the pros, the combat system is fun on its own when I don't have to reload my save because the enemy suddenly explodes on death or resurrects itself. The amount of combos and interactions between spells, surfaces, and items allows for a lot of freedom and tactics. Even if it boils down to nuking armor into CC, there are so many ways you can achieve your goals. Alongside the dumb Gotcha! fights, there are also a lot of great battles in this game which you can win by strategy, positioning and properly building your characters. The companions are quite interesting and the first two acts are significantly better than the latter two. The lizards are also my favorite non-human race of all RPGS by far.

Ultimately, I cannot recommend this game, mainly because of how unbalanced, inconsistent and riddled with clutter it is. I must admit that I enjoyed getting through the first half, but its glaring and impossible to ignore flaws become more apparent the further you go.
very tough start, act one took me 3 trys to get a good run going but past that 10/10 until the start of the final act, and the last fight were bs

Divinity: Original Sin 2 honestly surprised me with how much depth it has. I expected a good RPG, but I didn’t expect to lose hours just exploring, talking to random characters, and experimenting with different ways to handle quests. The game really rewards creativity, and it feels like there’s never just one “correct” solution to anything. The turn-based combat ended up being way more fun than I thought too. At first it seemed complicated, but once I got used to it, I was completely hooked. Using the environment during fights made every battle feel strategic instead of repetitive. Even small encounters could turn into complete chaos in the best way possible. The writing and companions were probably my favorite part though. The characters actually felt interesting and memorable, and I liked that your decisions affect relationships and the story in noticeable ways. It made me care more about what was happening instead of just rushing through quests. What really makes this game stand out to me is the amount of freedom it gives the player. You can approach situations however you want, and the game usually adapts to it instead of forcing you down one path. It’s rare to find an RPG that feels this open while still having a genuinely good story and fun gameplay. I’d for sure recommend it to anyone who likes story-heavy RPGs or games where your choices actually matter.

Cant believe i slept on this game so long this is the most fun ive had playing a video game in so long. Cant wait to play BG3 after this.

Larian Studios could make a game about a phone book and it would be a hit.
Runs fine on microsoft surface pro 9. Ensure your save location is to local drive, because cloud (onedrive) sync issues may cause game to crash if it spontaneously decides to fail.

Before playing Baldurs Gate 3 I tried playing the game but I just couldn't past the first part and stopped playing. After playing BG3 and experienced what Larian can do I said damn lets give it a try. This game is beatiful I didn't even finish the game but it is absolutely beatiful.
This game is not for people new to the genre. I was excited for the voiced story but even on the lowest difficulty it's way too hard without looking up guides.
Amazing turn-based RPG game and very addicting.

When it's good, it's great, when it's bad, it's the most annoying game in the world.
Still loved it, big recommend, yes

♥♥♥♥♥♥ игра багается каждые 10 минут, систематически страдаю от багов различной направленности и силы. Последний раз буквально минуту назад прочувствовал на себе 43 смертельных удара ножом в момент собственного хода использовав навык тактический отход. Из других проёбов разработчиков наличествует постоянный и практически бесперерывный баг, делающий мой навык замкнутая цепь похожим на красивый но абсолютно безвредный Ёбаный Фейрверк. Невидимые текстуры и упоминать не стоит, как и многое более мелкое но не менее раздражительное говно доброжелательно и радушно оставленное мне многоуважаемыми разрабами, очень их люблю как и всю их семью до третьего колена
Typical Larian win. Lots of great characters and absolute replayability. They don't miss.

75%
+ Interesting story
+ Great map design and character sheet choices
+ Challenging gameplay
- Lore can be overbearing
- Some main characters are forgettable
Excellent game. Music is incredible and the gameplay very rewarding
Great Story, Fun Multiplayer combat, and a rewarding combat system.

this game is really fun but wow its so much harder then BG3. I hope the next one can keep its almost cartoony style of combat and world interactions while managing to be easier for people to actuly play and progress like BG3. Im fiinally into act two and every big fight still feels like im playing the game totaly wrong and that feeling has gotten very old.

Divinity: Original Sin 2 is a fantastic game, and is absolutely worth playing after BG3. The story, gameplay, and RPG mechanics are all fantastic. Every single character has some gut-wrenching decisions, and the overall story is beautiful and gives the player a ton of autonomy. This game is as good as BG3, except for a few caveats. The biggest drawback is most dialogue is text based, and DOS2 doesn't have the very pretty cutscenes and graphics that BG3 has. Multiplayer is a little worse as well, as you cannot take turns alongside another player even if you're together in the initiative. There is a little jank, and some balance issues (especially in act 4!) Other than that, it's incredible experience that is just as good if not better than BG3.

barely at act two and so far my biggest complaint is the difficulty, which is my fault, as i decided it was a brilliant idea to start my first playthrough on tactician... dont do that. otherwise it is enjoyable so far, but if you come from BG3, you do feel that this is and "older" game.
Amazing game, I played it after BG3. The story, gameplay, everything is so engrossing. I prefer BG3 because it's more modern but this was definitely a treat to play.
Fantastic game and amazing soundtrack. This game is what got me into turn based games and is a must play if you like fantasy RPGs. The story and the world building is amazing. 10/10
Larian's storytelling and game design, along with the mix of old-school tabletop and video games, has always been top-notch.

Feels like the game lost its magic and charm of the first game. The crafting system is still a messy pile of ♥♥♥♥.

I modded in some extra classes for variety, opted to change into one of them. The mod, for whatever idiotic reason, deletes your inventory.

So I opted to just drop all those tagged gift bags they give you....only to find out they can't be dropped. Who is the ♥♥♥♥♥♥ that made that decision? I have to individually remove each and every item and then lose all the bags to make the swap.

Just completely killed any motivation I had to play the game. Even more than finding out you can't make your own characters to join the adventure - you're stuck with the diversity crew.

Also, you can only have one bag open and you can't mark something as wares and then put it in the wares bag......absolute lazy slop of an inventory system. No improvement from the last game.

I'm on my second playthrough and it's every bit as fantastic as I remember. I've wanted to replay for a while but Larian's announcement re 'Divinity' prompted me for a lore refresh. It's great all round - gameplay, narrative, voice acting, landscapes but Larian's world-building is deeply engaging. Highly recommended, especially with 'Divinity' in our future!

a great game if you enjoy strategic planning, sprawling fantasy adventure.
after doing a playthrough doing everything i possibly can, it really holds up compared to bg3. i really like having less rng and dice rolling; and hope larian still works around ap in the new divinity. 
cutscenes are much better in bg3 along with story / dialogue choice. in dos2 a lot of dialogue ends up being different flavors of how to say something, and some of the choices you make (specifically killing a certain someone near the end of act 1) really dont affect much beyond just persuading someone not to fight you.
all in all i give it 9/10 you really could just play this and bg3 with mods for the rest of your life
Its lovely. Difficult. Fun. Story is amazing. I absolutely love this game. It will be in my heart forever.

Personally I liked the first game much more , but that doesn't mean that this one is bad , I loved Act 1 so much , Act 2 was great , Act 3 was a bit short but still great and finally Act 4 was not really my favorite but overall 9/10.

i really enjoyed exploring in this game, it can be difficult and full of all kinds of creative fights and predicaments and sometimes the rewards may not always feel great there is yet still so much to love and discover

Great game. Narrative, gameplay, characters, and world were all fantastic. While BG3 has the game beat in presentation and cinematics, Larian is at their best when not restricted by the rules of DnD (both in terms of combat and worldbuilding).

Ending was clearly slightly rushed (in typical Larian fashion) although doesn't hurt the game in any way and still offers a satisfying end.
Good tactical. Creative character building. Challenging fights. I have played through almost 4 times now and still like the game.
Great game. Huge world with great main quests, and sidequests within sidequests. Love it!

Exceptionally immersive story, fighting gameplay, and physical environment, great voice acting, and rewards curiosity and exploration more than any other game I've played. It took some time to get used to things like the absent quest mapping and some absent QOL stuff that they added in BG3, but it wasn't at all enough to detract from the game. Story starts getting a tiny little bit contrived and bethesda-esque starting in Act 3, seemed like content might have been cut in order to wrap up the story. 

The steep difficulty jumps that can occur both within and between acts tends to encourage an xp-maxing murder goblin playstyle, but also made it feel more rewarding to slow down and meticulously choose and plan my fights since I wanted to avoid that kind of gameplay for RP reasons. Definitely a game where "cheesing" doesn't exist in my opinion, especially for the three hardest boss fights in Act 4.

The whole journey was just so cool. It feels like I just played an entire series of books. I think my very favorite part was the final fight in Act 3, where all the characters vying for godhood are pinned against each other in a battle royal race to the macguffin that bestows divinity.
Larian just makes great RPG's. The story is fun and immersive. Very fun to play co-op too.

Coming from BG3, I thought it was a little hard to get into at first, but the game definitely starts to get interesting after the first few levels, when you start to acquire more gear and unlock skills from totems and such. A couple things that I like a bit more is one: not having to worry about your party members feelings as much; and two: the freedom in terms of character building. Plays well on steam deck as expected.

7/10. even more ways to do the thing. a must play for those on the Baldurs Gate 3 pilgrimage. 

this game is a massive improvement in every way over D:OS except in the musical score, the sort of thing that only Pokrovsky could have produced. in most other regards D:OS2 is where you can see the true DNA of Baldurs Gate 3.

the combat is drastically improved, the dialogue and writing are better, and there are even more ways to do the thing. i defeated a demon lord by barricading his house, baiting him, and teleporting him outside it, separating him from all his buddies. i defeated a crazy undead dwarf inside a deathmine by doing roughly the same thing. i had no business winning either of those battles but Larian gave me a way.

to be clear, i NEVER used higher level abilities and rarely used Source. my skill books were full of 1st and 2nd tier skills. who needs 4th tier when your entire party all have heal, fortify, frost armor, teleport, haste, and several different movement skills. who needs high level combat skills when you have 2-3 archers spamming elemental and charm arrows?

moving on. the story, dialogue, writing, and voice acting are all drastically improved. there are even more branching stories and most of the endings also branch a lil bit on minor details depending on how you played the game. unlike D:OS there were several moments in this game that made me consider the ethical  consequences of the choices i made. by the end of the game i felt even more conflicted about the final decision i had to make. 

considering all that i still prefer the rawness and rough edges of D:OS. maybe its because it was my 1st Larian game. maybe its because D:OS had one of the best soundtracks of any media produced ever. maybe its because D:OS took itself even less seriously than D:OS2. this game takes itself WAY too seriously considering how the end of the game gets pretty corny depending on ur party comp. 

considering all that this game is great and its worth whatever price you can get it for. both D:OS and D:OS2 are games that i will certainly replay and would glady repay to experience for the 1st time again.

Overall, the game is great. The gameplay loop is easy to understand, fun to learn, and works just as well solo as it does with friends. If I'm being honest, anyone saying the game is too hard is probably dealing with a skill issue. I've played through it on multiple accounts and always treat Tactician as the minimum difficulty because it's the only mode that feels consistently engaging. Once you understand the mechanics, positioning, and how to build your characters, the difficulty becomes part of the fun rather than a roadblock.

The pacing can be a little slow at times, especially with random players, but that's a small price to pay for the amount of freedom and replayability the game offers. Every run can be completely different depending on your choices, builds, companions, and how much chaos you decide to cause. Whether you're trying out a new class, experimenting with weird combinations, or doing a run where you murder everyone except Buddy, there's always something new to experience.

I gotchu buddy,you a real one,the dawg in em is what makes him such a goat.
Great game, different than BG3, but the difference is a nice change of pace if that is your first experience with Larian.
Goated game. Pretty hard pick between this or BG3 being one of my favorite games of all time.

9.8/10
Great game, even with its flaws — still a very pleasant 185 hours.
The story is interesting, and the heroes are great — I really got attached to them. I played with a friend, and we noticed that the game doesn't feel very well built for co-op. This became especially noticeable near the end — or maybe it just seemed that way.
We also noticed that out of the six origin characters, Fane gets way more attention, both story-wise and gameplay-wise. He literally kills everyone in one turn thanks to his innate skill Time Warp, which lets him take two turns in one by the mid-game. Because of that, my characters sometimes didn't even get a turn. It got kind of boring. And story-wise — Fane is basically the main character. Which is weird for a game with six origin heroes. To learn the most interesting lore secrets and even part of the ending, you really only need him.
The endings were a bit disappointing too. Not bad — just average. None of them really made us feel anything, and we watched all of them.
Combat balance also felt off. At level 20, the most armor I could get was around 3,000 — and that was just magic armor. Meanwhile, enemies at the same level (not even bosses) often had 7,000 of both physical and magic armor. And they had abilities that strip all your armor in one hit and take half your health. Maybe it's by design — since at some point you become overpowered and the game gets too easy. Or maybe it's because the player has a brain and NPCs are just running scripts.
We also got stuck on quests a lot — had to ask the internet for help just to figure out where to go or what to do. Maybe that's on us. Maybe we're just bad at following clues.
Still, the game is excellent. You don't feel like pirating games like this from developers like this. Next on the list: Divinity 1, Baldur's Gate 3, and eventually Divinity 3 when it comes out and when we get around to it.
This was an incredible experience that me and my friend will never forget.

Voice acting is perfect! Story is perfect! It's very philosophical even though on the surface it looks like more simple good vs evil fairytale at first. Combat is not without issues, but I guess it's just ambitious what they've tried to achieve with it so It was difficult to balance. 
  
  Not huge but still a spoiler with critique: [spoiler]One issue is that the end (both on the ship and after) doesn't do justice to the buildup. It needs more with original characters, specifically their interaction with each other. There should've been an event/party at the end not just everybody standing by themselves and leaving on their own after. Frankly, I don't want all characters to leave, why can't some stay after all we have been through?[/spoiler]
  
  Also, please don't play on tactician/honor at least not the first time. Enjoy the story. No meta builds, lookup what to do on the Internet, tricks, exploits, hour long fights.
one of my favorite games to date. >>>>> bg3 btw. loved the combat. loved the story. a lot of replayability, especially for playing with different companions each run.

This was the game that got me into turn-based tactical RPGs. The combat system is similar to BG3 but it has an "Action Point" system that determines how much movement and what attacks/spells you can do. They have a cooldown period instead of spell slots. The way armour works is different too - you have to wear down armour types (physical or magical) with attacks instead of it just providing constant damage resistance. It's really fun. I also liked the companions -particularly the Red Prince- and quirky dialogue. The narrative is fun too but I will say the manner in which it wraps up the story is a bit underwhelming. Otherwise very fun.
It's a solid, really long RPG with challenging battles and puzzles and interesting characters, companion or NPC alike.

This is a great game. I'm not even finished with act 2 yet and I know this. It is a unique spin on the typical fantasy tales with some ambitious choices in design that make the experience new and exciting. The story definitely doesn't hold your hand at all and combat is something that requires serious thought. If you are just starting out, I do personally recommend beginning a campaign on explorer mode. Learn the games mechanics, immerse yourself in the world. Have fun :)

If you like Baldur's Gate 3 you will like this game! 
I bought this game after I grew tired of BG3 from playing it too much (800+ hours of playtime). At first the difference in graphics was a bit hard to adjust to, but the gameplay made up for it. Just like BG3 this game is quite addictive. It is a more challenging game than BG3, but I found DOS2 very fun after honor mode in BG3 no longer felt like a challenge. The writing is compelling and I just love exploring the world.
very fun. plays like their big game baulders gate. every immersive.
This game is a top tier RPG...it was made by Larian so that was  expected .  If you love Baldur's Gate 3 this is where the team cut it's teeth. excellence!

Larian is the GOAT, and Divinity: Original Sin 2 is their golden child. Yes, Baldur's Gate III is an epic adventure of mythic proportion, and Larian did it proud, but D:OS2 is pure Larian. With each of the origin characters, I find myself asking "What would this character do" instead of "what would I do," which is how I normally play games. The combat is highly satisfying and scratches my itch for tactical turn-based combat. I have forever lamented the move to real-time with pause starting in 1998. Our new embrace of turned based is really a welcome change. This game is amazing. Get it. You won't be disappointed.

Amazing game, way harder than BG3 but with a way more rewarding system. The story and amount of dialogue I think it's on the shorter side, but I feel that's because it's a way longer game, took me 88 hours to finish one playthrough, and granted, I've explored everything I could. 

The combat system is the greatest departure, it uses action points for everything , but the magic system is really creative , allwing you to combo elements for great fun and power.

Remember, everything is on fire, all the time.
Played about 10 hours and really liking it so far. Feels like a DnD adventure without the actual dice rolling. Great alternative to Baldur's Gate 3.

I played it after completing Baldur's Gate 3, even if the game is older and is missing a bunch of quality of life stuff from BG3 i was well used to, the game is great, combat is different but in a good and unique way with many spells, possible combos and team builds. 
And the AI can be surprisingly smart, in normal mode they are nerfed but in tactician mode they can do some really disgusting combo and destroy me, it was fun to have to think better against an AI made more difficult from doing smarter decision, instead of them doing more damage and having more health.
The maps are i think well made with secret places there and there rewarding exploration, and graphically it's still pleasing to look at, very similar to BG3.
I enjoyed this game a lot, enough to get all achievements for it! 
I'm excited to see what Larian will do for the next Divinity game!

I came for “turn-based RPG with good reviews” and left 120 hours later emotionally attached to a skeleton and every random NPC I accidentally set on fire.

DOS2 is chaos, freedom, and some of the best co-op RPG gameplay ever made.

Can be very challenging, both combat and going about missions in the right way to get maximum rewards. Used the walkthrough several times.
Absolutely huge fun though!

This game is graphically beautiful, the writing and characters are excellent, the core turn-based combat is incredible, and in many aspects it's bigger than the first game, however, being bigger doesn't necessarily mean always being better.
This is perhaps the most frustrating RPG I've ever played in terms of mechanics.

To summarize in a few points:

1) Shield mechanic: I'm not a big fan, mainly because it stops CC and makes combat primarily focused on damage, which makes several abilities irrelevant.

2) Combat design: It's full of gimmicks and annoying mechanics, combined with the fact that the combat locations are large and enemies jump and teleport everywhere, which makes the fights irritating and time-consuming.

3) The RPG system and balancing: I'm not a big fan of the attributes and abilities, basically some choices are clearly overpowered and it seems that the combat was balanced expecting the player to use cheesy strategies, which leaves the choice very limited to what is "correct" and OP. 

I can't leave a negative review for this game; it's clearly a labor of love, and I really enjoyed the characters and stories.

After playing BG3 (on another account), I can say that they improved on the points mentioned above, and I'm excited for the next Divinity. 🧀🧙👍🏼

Divinity: Original Sin 2 is an absolute masterpiece! If you loved Baldur’s Gate 3, you’re going to enjoy this one a lot. It has the same incredible depth, creative combat, and freedom that makes Larian’s games so special.
Sure, it doesn’t have the cinematic video scenes that BG3 offers, but that’s completely understandable — this is an older game, and it more than makes up for it with its amazing writing, reactivity, and gameplay.
I can’t wait to see what Larian Studios brings us next. In a gaming world full of battle passes, game passes, and companies focused only on squeezing more money out of players, Larian continues to deliver genuine, high-quality, passion-driven experiences. That’s why they have so much respect from the community.
Thank you for this amazing piece of entertainment!

Games pretty fun even with mods. Spent 50 mins on 1 battle cause 3 of us are being too dumb and don't know what to do. It's fun when you and your friends spend the time to get down and play it, getting everyone to agree on a day and time is like setting up a DnD session lol.
Side note: Literally had this moment where the enemies and us were slipping on ice for good chunk
One of the best games I've ever played, never played before BG3 and glad I picked this up recently and went through it all.
played because I liked BG3. This is better in every way besides cinematics. absolute masterpiece.

Very good game. Especially the part where the game was going to be text only, and you went in with full voice acting. That was a huge bet, saw the backstory as to how big the gamble was.

My first playthrough was with red prince, his arrogant and over the top aristocracy is like cream to pudding.

Divinity 2 was so much fun to play with a group of friends. I'm not normally a friend of party RPGs, I like playing a single character only, so the co-op was the perfect fit for me. I liked how much depth there is to the customization of even just one class. I didn't care much for the story, in fact I can't even remember most of it (I think you are the chosen one or something). But the mechanics and feeling of adventure are good enough to carry the game on their own. I'd recommend this if you have a group to enjoy it with.

Decided to play this and the first Divinity Original Sin after playing Larian's Baldurs Gate 3. This game is just as satisfying with both role play and action to engage any type of player with a CRPG type game. Excellent for anyone to play.

The game is great in graphics and creative possibilities of solving problems/battles, but would benefit from a more linear and clear quest structure - going back and forth constantly and wondering which step makes the most sense first is taking up too much time. Also some kills will not be reflected in the story (killed bunch of unimportant people who were mentioned in the end as survivors anyways). Also some characters just killed themselves by accident, which is funny but unwanted.

At some point it is very tiring to go through all the dialogues that aren't interesting, leading me to rather kill and be done with it quickly. Too many collectibles, crafting etc - thankfully it is absolutely necessary to craft something only about five times. The story was fine before it got too complicated, in short - everyone is a bad guy so you are left only with choosing the lesser evil or going the evil path. Main characters are okay - could be better, could be worse. Music and sounds are great and contribute to immersion into the game. All in all, I would play it again, had it not been such a tedious long way. Once is fine, once is enough.
A melhor adaptação que ja fizeram de um rpg de mesa pra um jogo. É exatamente a sensação de sentar e jogar com seus amigos num sábado a tarde

Quality of detail is next level. If you like Baulders gate or similar games, you will love this. You're given alot of freedom of choice which i prefer way more then being railroaded through an RPG and every quest feels like a chore. Definetly not the case here. The customization of your characters and the gear they wear is vastly expansive. The diaglogue voice acting is better than 97% of the acting on Netflix these days. I look forward to putting many more hours into this game. And you should to.

Wird seinen Lorbeeren gerecht. Ein spielerisch und taktisch enorm vielfältiges CRPG mit hoher atmosphärischer Dichte. Wer gerne an jedem Ecken etwas neues entdeckt, sich in seine Charaktere vertieft und allerlei Lösungswege und taktische Möglichkeiten austüftelt, ist hier goldrichtig.
Great game, I've currently only played it with my brother and it's been a ton of fun. We got a mod as well that added to the combat and we just keep playing!

Oil rig didn't frustrate me

Game has really nice intreractive environment and rewards creative thinking

Some of the last bosses, though, are quite fat and it's not really fun to fight them
its like baulders gate but battle is simpler some times less is more

This game is insanely fun. I jumped in “just to try it out” and suddenly realized I’d been playing for hours without noticing. The story pulls you in, the choices feel meaningful, and the combat is super satisfying.

If you love classic RPGs, this is basically a dream come true. It nails that old-school vibe but still feels fresh and modern. Every decision matters, every fight keeps you thinking, and every session makes you want to play just a little longer… and then a lot longer.

10/10 would lose track of time again.

People with ADHD, don't buy this game. There is too much to do, everywhere, Many places you have to come back later to, when you're stronger or when you've found some artifact/key or when you've figured out what to do there. I, for one, need a more linear game. No way to distinguish what's really important from what's only an easter egg (except of course the "main quest"/"side quests" distinction in the quest log). I couldn't even get to the point where I leave the first map. Alas, I also have a 100% completion syndrome :]

But of course I'll recommend it. A feature that has me completely lost will be an asset for other players.

After more than 100 hours, I can say this is one of the best games I've ever played. The story is wonderful and deep, the characters are meticulously written, the combat system is enjoyable, and the interaction with the world is fantastic. A truly great game.
huge story, would be hard as heck to get the same run through twice. Lots of ways to handle fights.
Great turn based strategy game with D&D like elements

Playing this game is like getting cozy in your favorite fuzzy blanket with your favorite fantasy novel next to a hearth, and reading the day away. The controls are very similar to BG3, so it was super comfortable to jump into it, and I'm thoroughly enjoying the story. Makes me so happy that BG3 wasn't a fluke. Larian has created a beautiful standard for tactical RPGs, and I am excited to play more from their library after finishing DoS2.

Pros:
- Gameplay is clean just like in BG3- super satisfying to kill the bad guys (and/or good guys)
- Story is gripping. Fort Joy is an incredible opening act for the game. I am almost done with Act 2, but that has been splendid so far as well. Excited to see how it wraps up.
- Characters are well-written. I liked almost the entire cast immediately- they're voiced well and they all have unique motivations and stories.
- This soundtrack SLAPS. My kids have to listen to someone absolutely killing it on the bansuri now when I drive them to school.

Cons:
- No "cutscenes" - this is only a serious con if you were expecting the same exact thing as BG3. Luckily, I was warned that this was not the case, and this barely registers as a negative to me.
- Everything is on fire. The floor is on fire. My characters are on fire. My keyboard is on fire, please help me. (They could've toned down surface related fire/explosions/etc. Things can get very chaotic, which honestly might be a plus for some people)
- Easy to miss certain scenes/content. Because no cutscenes, you have to be very vigilant about talking to your party FREQUENTLY. It's easy to miss big conversations which can have an impact on story.

Overall: 9/10
Super fantastic game. This has cemented Larian as one of the greatest game studios of our generation.
Great game, this is my second play through in the past several years and it has a lot of challenges, great story, and interesting quests.

The game itself is fine/fun - but sometimes kinda rough around the edges. I would probably give 3,5 out of 5 points.

Examples:

Several times innocent NPCs are about to get killed. In theory the player would have plenty of ways to prevent that - but scripts kill off the NPCs anyway. With attacks even going through walls etc.. This gets kinda annoying since no matter the skill and progress, the player can´t do anything at all.

Several stories from quests don´t feel finished. Like some NPCs asking you to find/help their mates/family etc... After finding them they don´t get reunited though. The story/quest ends without the (happy) ending. 
Same with sickness - finding sick NPCs with a sad backgroundstory- but you can´t help. You just leave them behind - quest finished...

Just leaving a zone will remove quest-endings. For no reason whatsoever.. The quest didn´t progress, nothing changed- same old NPCs in the same place doing the same thing. It is frustrating as player since u don´t get any warning/info before leaving the zone. It just wastes the game design that went into the creation of those quests - for no reason

This game seemed great but it definitely couldn't run on my sh*tty macbook air! Gameplay was great but my framerate was bad and I could hardly run graphics! If you have any decent equipment to run it on I recommend!

So I tried it for the first time, and it’s a great game. Don’t be afraid of the reviews — it was really well made. The game had great visuals for its time, a good story, and a lot of storylines. The gameplay is the best part of this game. The interactions, magic, and effects are so great, and you have a lot of combinations for fighting enemies. If you played Baldur’s Gate 3, this game feels like its father because it has many gameplay mechanics that you can’t even see in BG3. But I stopped near the end. I did the same thing with BG3 — I just got bored. Still, the game is great, so maybe the problem is me.
Game is outstanding. The follower quests are all incredible, its refreshingly difficult, and youll find yourself coming to after a 6 hour binge not knowing where the time went.

Après 120h, ça y est c'est le moment d'écrire mon avis. 

Divinity... Quel pied. Je ne te connaissais pas, j'étais orphelin de ton grand frère BG3 et je ne savais pas quel jeu faire. Tout me paraissait fade. 
Puis, j'ai choisi de découvrir le passé de Larian. 

Graphiquement, aucun soucis en 2026, c'est toujours très beau. 

Niveau gameplay, on est pas sur du pur D&D donc, je pense que c'est encore plus simple pour les novices (comme moi) du jeu de plateau. 

Et niveau histoire, récit, personnage, narration, choix, conséquences... C'est toujours aussi bon, solide. 

Un réel jeu qui domine son genre.

I haven't reached Act 3 yet, but this videogame is on the road to become my FAV of all time. A few months back it was simply unthinkable to be able to find a game that would make me immerse so much in its story, characters, world, mechanics, etc. I highly recommend it, even if its your first turn-based (it was for me), took me only a 2nd playthrough to figure most things out, which isn't bad at all.

One of the best cRPGs ever made. Such a unique and fun combat system, personally I think it is much more fun than BG3's D&D system. Very compelling and interesting storylines of the original characters worth trying different choices to see what would happen and how they affect the later chapters. 100% recommend this game.
Great game. However the LAST FIGHT.... nearly caused me to throw my computer out a window. Highly recommend
Love this game. one of the greatest RPGs that exist. give it a few hours and you will love it. The learning curve is a bit steep but once you hit your flow its amazing
Lots of different options.  I have played the game for year, and still enjoy playing.  If you like turn based role playing, this is a winner.

Started this because a colleague of mine said it might be a game I’d enjoy, and I ended up putting over 100 hours into it within the first month while loving every second of it.

The story is good, but the real highlight for me is the gameplay. It’s simple to learn but still rewards skill and understanding of combos to maximize your damage and make you feel incredibly powerful.

There are also plenty of side quests and a huge variety of builds to experiment with. My favorite is the Pyromancer because, with the right preparation, you can often wipe out entire fights in a single turn. 

I’d highly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys turn-based combat and fantasy settings.

Great game, complex and long but worth it. Good amount of choices and puzzles. Fairly difficult but managable. We played it locally together. Recommended :)

One of the greatest games of all time.

This review is post Baldurs Gate 3 but i've played the game both before and after BG3 released. I think Baldurs gate is probably their stronger product and beats out Divinity in depth and character interactions as well as diversity of playthroughs. However where i think Divinity really shines is it's combat. While both games are inspired by D&D, Baldurs gate takes it a step too far into trying to be like the pen and paper game. Divinity combat is fast and loose allowing you to pull off massive attack chains and spell combos due to it's AP system where in baldurs gate if you choose any sort of casting build you are usually limited to 1-2 spells per turn. If you enjoyed Baldurs gate and want more of the same or maybe are looking for a little more engaging combat then divinity 2 is the answer. I look forward to Divinity 3 in hopes it will do everything baldurs gate did better but keep the combat of divinity 2.

Best RPG ever. I like BG3, but I really hope the new one is closer in tone to this game than BG.
At least it won't have Aylin, which is already an extra point in its favor.

By the infamous creators of Divinity Original Sin Enhanced Edition, this game is marvelous. I felt a breath of fresh air playing this and thoroughly enjoyed not only the amazing writing but also the creativity behind this game. Stemming from Baldur's Gate 3, I have decided to try the previous Larian DOS games due to the high praise for their series and I was not let down. I consider Divinity Original Sin 2 on the same level of depth and storytelling as Baldur's Gate 3. There were some small issues I've had throughout the game but it was overshadowed by the sheer detail in this game. I loved this game as a whole and consider it equal to Baldur's Gate 3.
Just play the game, its fun af. Requires just a little bit of strategy sometimes when fighting and gives you multiple options to achieve the main goal of each arc.

This game is an absolute blast, there is great freedom with class building and the combat is quite nice

Combat might be hard when one starts out first, but the more knowledge you get, the more efficiently you will make combat choices 

Great story!
I love it when we can literally break the game mechanics but the game still progressed like nothing happened.

Bought this game on discount and wanted to give it a try(never played games like this before) and all i can say is i got hooked soo deep...
Turn based gameplay is nice.
Many ways to interact with world and to deal with enemies in combat.
Story/Lore was deep.

Only negative thing about this game is all the cryptic ways to progress the story. I know, it is an RPG game but i had to pull up a guide a few times too many in order to get anywhere. Maybe it's just me...

For those that have played Baldur's Gate 3, this will feel very familiar. I do suggest playing Divinity Original Sin 1 before this one, as this has an improved interface. Baldur's Gate 3 is then another improvement upon this. 
Story wise you can start with this one, as there is a significant time jump and society in game has changed drastically. There are nods to the first one, and certain things are mentioned, but it is it's own game completely. 
The mechanics of the game are similar to both above mentioned games. I'm not a huge fan of the way physical and magic armor works as I feel it limits how you build your team, and I miss the ability to turn movement turn based any time I want along with being able to click on a slot and have all items I can equip be listed. (this from BG3).
Regardless, super fun game with some interesting interactions and another story in a cool world. I'm really looking forward to DoS3 in a few years time.
Cant play the game as stupid sexy lizard 10/10 lizard everything else is alright too =[
Played this after baldurs gate 3 and I cant wait for div 3 to launch. Awesome game,

game so good you'll try to play with friends but end up beating it alone because of scheduling conflicts

10/10 Larian Games BBEG is always having free time to play with friends

Rating 9/10 (only because its compared to BG3/Baldur's gate 3)
Pros:
- Combat is 10/10, love how easy it is to learn new abilities that have different skil requirements, and allows for very flexible builds. The combat can be very deep with loads of approaches. The fights are very well designed, allowing you to cheese them, or to make things easier for yourself if you position correctly or target the right people.
- The elements play so well in this game and make fights so interesting, you can put out a fire to create steam that hides you, if you can't target someone in steam, you can electrify it to make it damage them
- Choices matter RPG, this should be the standard of RPGs, NPCs remember what you did to them, they chase you if you steal from them, they aggro you if you are poking around in the wrong places etc. TLDR feels very real and responsive to you, which makes it so immersive
- World is so expansive and so much to explore 
- Interesting story once I sat down and learned the lore
- Ability to respec at all times, trying different abilities strategies, and builds
- Typical passion and expansiveness you can expect from Larian, these guys create games like no other
Cons:
- Traditional CRPG text-based story telling is confusing to follow, and easy to get lost
- Last boss fight was very underwhelming and bs, forces you down a very specific approach with unavoidable damage, very unlike most other fights in this game
- "Sebille shares lore with Ifan", wtf does this mean? and in comparison to bg3 where you have to manage how you react to the game so your party either like you more or less is not present, your party's lore characters have their own stories but they don't really interact with you, just to their own stories.
More content than BG3, but less polish. Still, an amazing game!

An older game I've had in my library for ages. Probably got it on sale. Should have played it sooner. The indivisual stories have depth. I'm quite enjoying talkng to people. Playing more of the story rather than the fighting.  +++

The greatest quest of the game, figuring out which characters you want in your party and getting the music out of your head once you hear it. Both though are excellent, along with every pixel of the game, it goes on sale constantly but even if you pay full price it's worth every penny.
